title: aira major subsp. cespitosa
intro: Aira major subsp. cespitosa, also known as rough meadow-grass, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Aira major subsp. cespitosa include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve menstrual cramps, and calm the nervous system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
